LinkedIn and 3rd parties use essential and non-essential cookies to provide, secure, analyze and improve our Services, and to show you relevant ads (including professional and job ads) on and off LinkedIn. Learn more in our Cookie Policy.

Select Accept to consent or Reject to decline non-essential cookies for this use. You can update your choices at any time in your settings.

Job Application Email Tips for [Job Title] Role

This title was summarized by AI from the post below.
Sarah Adam
5mo
  • Report this post

Never Just Send Your CV When applying for a job via email, don’t just attach your CV with no message Your email is part of your first impression. Here’s a simple, professional way to write it: Subject: Application for the [Job Title] Role Hi [Hiring Manager's Name], I hope you're doing well. I'm writing to apply for the [Job Title] position you recently posted. I’ve attached my CV for your review. I believe my skills and background make me a good fit for this role, and I’d love the opportunity to be part of your team. Looking forward to hearing from you. Best regards, [Your Full Name] [Your Phone Number] [Your Email Address] ✅ Quick Reminders: Always send your CV as a PDF (never as a photo or screenshot). Check your spelling and grammar. Keep it polite and simple, and no need for long paragraphs. Use a clear subject line like: Application for [Job Title]. #CV #resume #Saudi #Careers #HR #Jobs #Hiring #Security #وظائف #Technology #Network #Engineers #Python #Data ------------------------------------ Share Your CV and Get More Job Opportunities www.hiresme.

1,121 63 Comments

But honestly, Did the HR have the time to read that brief email which is generally wrote by AI. I think that they should focus on CV reading.

CV / resume has less than 20% impact. It's all about networking and who you know. Everyone is pushing AI initiative which is great, but bottom line, if you know someone, you will get the job.

How do you recommend outreach after linkedin applications are submitted? In addition, as a US-based attorney, I was advised I will have better luck securing employment if I am on the ground and present in Riyadh, etc. If I were to spend three weeks in Riyadh to make connections and meet people, how do you suggest I target the right people and how would you recommend approaching them? Do you offer consultations---really looking for some solid guidance. Will send you a message

26 Reactions 27 Reactions
Faizan Bashir 4mo
  • Report this comment

Cover Letter Dear Hiring Manager, I am writing to express my interest in the position of Civil Supervisor (Building Works) in your esteemed organization. With over 12 years of hands-on experience in civil construction supervision, I have developed strong expertise in managing building projects from foundation to finishing, ensuring quality, safety, and timely completion. Throughout my career, I have supervised a wide range of projects including residential, commercial, and institutional buildings. My core responsibilities have included site supervision, contractor coordination, material inspection, quantity verification, and ensuring compliance with approved drawings and technical specifications. I am well-versed in monitoring daily site activities, maintaining progress reports, and implementing safety standards in line with company and regulatory requirements. I possess strong leadership and problem-solving skills, enabling me to handle site challenges efficiently while maintaining. Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to the possibility of an interview. Sincerely, [Faizan Bashir] Civil Supervisor – Building Works 13 Years’ Experience [+966591598153] faizanbashir524@gmail.com]

Reply

Jobvio 5mo
  • Report this comment

Further every CV should bei adjusted individually to the job. Especially watch out for ATS optimization. An easy trick which brings you way more invited because otherwise your filtered out way to early :/

Mohammad Khan 5mo
  • Report this comment

🔌 We’re Hiring: Electrical Engineer – Industrial Power Systems & Estimation We are looking for an experienced Electrical Engineer to support bidding, estimation, and technical review for an industrial electrical project involving MV/LV systems, substations, transformers (up to 2 MVA), and diesel generators. Key Responsibilities: Prepare BOQs, cost estimates, and tender submissions Review electrical drawings, RFQs, and specifications Power cable sizing (gensets to panels) Technical coordination for generator and ventilation systems Requirements: Bachelor’s degree in Electrical Engineering Proven experience in industrial electrical projects Strong background in estimation and tendering Experience with substations, RMUs, transformers, and gensets Mandatory Requirement: ✅ Valid Saudi IQAMA with minimum 3 months validity 📩 Interested candidates, please send your CV via WhatsApp: 0595001320

    Never Just Send Your CV When applying for a job via email, don't just attach your CV with no message Your email is part of your first impression. Here's a simple, professional way to write it: Subject: Application for the [Job Title] Role Hi [Hiring Manager's Name], I hope you're doing well. I'm writing to apply for the [Job Title] position you recently posted. I've attached my CV for your review. I believe my skills and background make me a good fit for this role, and I'd love the opportunity to be part of your team. Looking forward to hearing from you. Best regards, [Your Full Name] [Your Phone Number] [Your Email Address] ✓ Quick Reminders: Always send your CV as a PDF (never as a photo or screenshot). Check your spelling and grammar.     Never Just Send Your CV When applying for a job via email, don’t just attach your CV with no message Your email is part of your first impression. Here’s a simple, professional way to write it: Subject: Application for the [Job Title] Role Hi [Hiring Manager's Name], I hope you're doing well. I'm writing to apply for the [Job Title] position you recently posted. I’ve attached my CV for your review. I believe my skills and background make me a good fit for this role, and I’d love the opportunity to be part of your team. Looking forward to hearing from you.   • Megha K.
    4mo
    • Report this post

    Your Resume Is Your First Impression — Make It Count. As a recruiter, I often see talented candidates miss good opportunities simply because their resume is not updated. Your resume is not just a document — it is your personal marketing tool. Why maintaining it regularly matters: 1️⃣ Opportunities Don’t Wait Recruiters may reach out anytime. An outdated resume may miss your latest achievements, skills, or promotions. 2️⃣ You Forget Your Own Achievements Updating only after 2–3 years makes you forget: Projects, tools, impact, and metrics. Updating every 3–6 months helps track your growth. 3️⃣ Promotions & Role Changes Matter New responsibilities and title changes clearly reflect career progression. 4️⃣ Add Numbers, Not Just Responsibilities Instead of “Handled database management” Write: “Managed MySQL production databases handling 5TB data, ensuring 99.9% uptime.” Numbers add credibility. 5️⃣ Keep Skills Relevant Technology evolves. Remove outdated skills and add new tools, certifications, trainings, and improvements. ✅ Quick Resume Checklist: ✔ Updated designation & company ✔ Latest projects added ✔ Measurable achievements included ✔ Irrelevant skills removed ✔ Grammar & formatting checked Your resume should reflect the current version of you, not the one from two years ago. A small habit of updating it regularly can open big career doors. #Hiring #Recruitment #ResumeTips #JobSearch

    33
    Like Comment

    To view or add a comment, sign in

  • Balkrishna Tiwari
    4mo
    • Report this post

    This resume just scored 95 ATS — but the score isn’t the real achievement. The real achievement was understanding how recruiters actually read resumes. Most resumes fail not because the person lacks skills… They fail because the information is written for humans, not for hiring systems. Here’s what I changed 👇 🔹 1. Role-focused resume Not “Data Science + Web + Java + Everything” One resume → One target role 🔹 2. Keywords matter more than design ATS searches skills written exactly like job descriptions: Machine Learning ✔ ML techniques ✖ 🔹 3. Projects = Results, not steps Bad: Built a model using Python Good: Developed prediction model achieving 92% accuracy and reduced manual effort by 40% 🔹 4. Remove certificate dumping Recruiters don’t hire certificates They hire problem solvers 🔹 5. Numbers create credibility Accuracy Latency improvement Dataset size Time saved 🔹 6. Skills section strategy Languages → Tools → Frameworks → Concepts (Not random lists) 🔹 7. No paragraphs Recruiters scan in 7–10 seconds 🔹 8. ATS friendly formatting No tables No icons No columns Simple structure 🔹 9. Each bullet answers What problem → What action → What impact 🔹 10. Resume is a marketing document Not your biography If you’re preparing for placements or job switch — Don’t just make a resume. Engineer it. Sharing mine for reference — hope it helps someone improve theirs 🚀

    6
    Like Comment

    To view or add a comment, sign in

  • Gautam D.
    5mo
    • Report this post

    Most resumes are garbage. Here’s the 80/20 of what makes a great resume: - Make sure your titles are the same for all your experiences. If you're applying for ML Engineering roles, ideally all of your previous roles should say "Machine Learning Engineer" as the title. Recruiters often look for this. - Make sure every single bullet point has quantitative metrics, like "increased accuracy by 30%". Recruiters automatically skip over bullet points that don't have metrics. - A lot of people are afraid to do this, but it's completely fine to cram keywords at the bottom of your resume. If you've used a technology at least once, add it to your resume. - If your projects don't have links to demos, you're not gonna make it. You need to include links to your GitHub profile and your portfolio. P.S. If you want me to personally evaluate your resume based on the principles that got me into Google, just book a free strategy call with me here: https://lnkd.in/ekU9wjGd

  • Mayank Singh
    4mo
    • Report this post

    💡 𝐓𝐡𝐞 𝟐𝟎𝟐𝟔 𝐑𝐞𝐬𝐮𝐦𝐞 𝐑𝐞𝐚𝐥𝐢𝐭𝐲: 𝐈𝐬 𝐚 𝐛𝐨𝐭 𝐛𝐥𝐨𝐜𝐤𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐲𝐨𝐮𝐫 𝐜𝐚𝐫𝐞𝐞𝐫? I’m thrilled to share that I just finished optimising my resume, and it officially hit a 𝟏𝟎𝟎% 𝐀𝐓𝐒 𝐜𝐨𝐦𝐩𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐛𝐢𝐥𝐢𝐭𝐲 𝐬𝐜𝐨𝐫𝐞! 🚀 Did you know that for 99% of large companies, your first interview isn't with a human—it's with an 𝐚𝐥𝐠𝐨𝐫𝐢𝐭𝐡𝐦? I recently optimised my Data Science & ML resume to a 100% ATS score, and the process taught me that what looks good to a human often looks like gibberish to a machine. Here are the Pro Tips I used to beat the gatekeeper: • 𝐓𝐡𝐞 "𝐄𝐱𝐚𝐜𝐭 𝐌𝐚𝐭𝐜𝐡" 𝐑𝐮𝐥𝐞:If a job description asks for "Project Management," don't write "Managed Projects." Parsers look for exact phrasing matches. • 𝐒𝐭𝐚𝐧𝐝𝐚𝐫𝐝𝐢𝐳𝐞 𝐘𝐨𝐮𝐫 𝐃𝐚𝐭𝐞𝐬:Use the Month YYYY – Month YYYY format. Inconsistent dating can cause "parsing errors," leading to an automatic low confidence score. • 𝐎𝐧𝐞 𝐂𝐨𝐥𝐮𝐦𝐧 𝐎𝐧𝐥𝐲:Multi-column layouts often cause the ATS to read text from left to right across the page, turning your resume into a jumbled mess of words. • 𝐅𝐢𝐥𝐞 𝐅𝐨𝐫𝐦𝐚𝐭 𝐢𝐬 𝐂𝐫𝐢𝐭𝐢𝐜𝐚𝐥:Use .docx for the most accurate parsing unless a PDF is specifically requested. Stop letting "formatting" hold you back from your dream role. Check your score today! #ATSResume #ATS #ResumeOptimization

    Like Comment

  • Maven Technologies LLC (Maventeck)

    952 followers

    4mo
    • Report this post

    A winning CV requires more than just listing your experience — it should clearly show your value and impact. Here’s what makes a CV stand out: 1️⃣ Clear Professional Summary Start with a short, powerful summary (3–4 lines) highlighting: Your core expertise Years of experience Key achievements Career focus 2️⃣ Strong Achievements (Not Just Responsibilities) Instead of: Responsible for managing projects Write: Led 5+ projects, improving delivery time by 30% and reducing costs by 15%. Use numbers wherever possible. 3️⃣ Relevant Skills Section Add skills aligned with the job role: Technical Skills (e.g., Java, AI, Cloud, Data Analytics) Tools & Technologies Soft Skills (Leadership, Communication, Problem-Solving) 4️⃣ Clean & Professional Format Simple layout Consistent font Bullet points 1–2 pages maximum 5️⃣ Keywords for ATS Optimization Many companies use Applicant Tracking Systems (ATS). Match your CV keywords with the job description. 6️⃣ Certifications & Continuous Learning Highlight relevant certifications (e.g., Cloud, AI, Salesforce, Cybersecurity). 7️⃣ Projects & Portfolio Especially important for Tech, AI, Data, and Developer roles. 8️⃣ Error-Free Content No spelling mistakes. No grammatical errors. #maventeck #WinningCV #ResumeTips #CareerGrowth #JobSearch #CareerSuccess #ProfessionalDevelopment #ResumeWriting #CareerAdvice #JobReady #InterviewPreparation #PersonalBranding #HireSmart #TalentAcquisition #ATS #CareerGoals
